body_txt: Nudity. It's Forbidden Fruit , it's Fanservice , but most importantly, it's funny. There's just something hilarious about somebody winding up naked, at least in a situation where it's not expected or wanted, and seeing the prudish costar react. Nudity has been a prominent ingredient in humor for at least 3,000 years . Even the ancient Egyptians found naked people funny, and they weren't themselves shy about wearing little more than loincloths . So maybe the humor lies primarily with boobs , butts , and penises , which have been shameful to expose since Neolithic times. After all, pushing boundaries and playing with taboos is a time-honored formula for humor, not to mention the added potential for a little titillation to giggle about . A character who ends up naked (or at least in only their underwear ) in a situation they don't expect may have a Naked Freak-Out and need to employ a Hand-or-Object Underwear . And if not, their naughty bits may still be hidden — from the camera, that is — by other forms of Censorship Tropes such as Scenery Censor (bonus points if it is a Gag Censor ). Meanwhile, the audience will have to make do with, at best, a Toplessness from the Back or Shoulders-Up Nudity shot. This is partly because the suggestion of nudity is often funnier than the full monty , and most media does have to keep things Safe For Work — or at least put a fig leaf on the idea. The Invisible Main Character probably can't wear clothes, if only because the insinuation that he or she is running around naked makes it funnier — plus when the character does inevitably materialize, it'll invariably be in front of as many people as possible. The Innocent Fanservice Girl won't understand why everyone's freaking out, while the Shameless Fanservice Girl won't care. Ms. Fanservice and The Vamp will understand, but... that's the idea. This is very often played for Cringe Comedy . A common setup involves a person who is comfortable in their own skin (such as a nudist ) having a run-in with a more squeamish onlooker. Or a prudish person might be embarrassed by getting caught without their clothes. Or a person might assume it's an appropriate time to strip down, only to find out they misread the situation (what do you mean, this isn't a nude beach?). Almost always, they'll end up meeting another character , ideally someone they didn't want to run into. The humor might also come from Fan Disservice , when the object is someone we really didn't want to see naked. Of course, someone is almost certain to remark, " Please Put Some Clothes On !" For specific examples of nudity as humor, see: Compare Comedic Underwear Exposure . Contrast Full-Frontal Assault . Especially common during Sweeps . May overlap Chivalrous Shirt . See also Nudism , which is very often Played for Laughs in media due to said Cringe Comedy potential.
